data.title: Wall Street volatility spikes as markets tumble amid credit concerns
data.description:
The Cboe Volatility Index (VIX), widely regarded as the market’s “fear gauge,” climbed above 27 on Friday, marking its highest level since April, as investor anxiety spiked amid concerns over the U.S. economy. Loan defaults at two regional banks fueled worries and prompted traders to take profits.

Zions Bancorporation (ZION) dropped 13% on Thursday after reporting a charge-off on a loan issued by a subsidiary, while Western Alliance Bancorp (WAL) fell 11% after disclosing loans to the same borrowers.

The developments echoed memories of the 2023 banking crisis, which started with Silicon Valley Bank’s collapse.

Investors are also watching the private credit sector closely following the failures of subprime auto lender Tricolor Holdings and auto parts supplier First Brands Group. Major banks, including JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) and Jefferies Financial Group (JEF), reported losses linked to these cases.

Banking stocks were set to extend losses on Friday, with Bank of America (BAC) down 1.6% in premarket trading and Morgan Stanley (MS) falling 1.3%.

As a result, stock index futures opened lower, with S&P 500 futures (SPX) down 0.9% following a 1% decline on Thursday, while Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) slipped 1.2%. Nvidia (NVDA) and Tesla (TSLA) were both off more than 2% in premarket trading.

Rising U.S.-China trade tensions are also weighing on sentiment, driving profit-taking after the S&P 500 (SP500) rallied 35% from its April low to an October high. Traders increased Treasury holdings on Friday, pushing 10-year (US10Y) yields down 3 basis points, while gold hit a fresh record high.

Here are some exchange-traded funds focused on volatility: (BATS:VXX), (BATS:VIXY), (VIXX), (BATS:VXZ), (BATS:VIXM), (BATS:UVXY), (BATS:SVXY), and (BATS:UVIX).

data.title: Schlumberger Non-GAAP EPS of $0.69 beats by $0.02, revenue of $8.93B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Schlumberger press release (NYSE:SLB): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.69 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $8.93B (-2.5% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Cash flow from operations was $1.68 billion and free cash flow was $1.10 billion, including $153 million of acquisition-related payments
  • Board approved quarterly cash dividend of $0.285 per share

data.title: Luminar Technologies is on watch with its founder considering a merger transaction
data.description:
Luminar Technologies (NASDAQ:LAZR) disclosed that founder Austin Russell holds an activist stake of 8.4% in the company. 

Per the SEC filing, Russell, at the suggestion of certain shareholders and the invitation of certain members of the board of directors of Luminar Technologies, sent a non-binding proposal to the board as a preliminary basis for discussion regarding a potential transaction in which Russell AI Labs would acquire 100% of the outstanding shares of the Class A stock of the company.

Russell AI Labs may concurrently acquire a different, larger global automotive technology company, and integrate such business into Luminar (NASDAQ:LAZR) to create a unified technology platform business, referred to as Luminar 2.0. The proposed transaction would see shareholders of the company would receive consideration of cash and/or marketable securities of the combined Luminar 2.0 entity. In connection with the proposed transaction, Russell AI Labs or other entities affiliated with Austin Russell may also consider investment of new capital in the combined company, subject to final terms of the deal.

Austin Russell resigned as president, CEO, and chairperson of Luminar Technologies on May 14 following a business conduct and ethics inquiry by the audit committee of the board of directors

Shares of Luminar Technologies (LAZR) rose 2.2% in premarket trading to $1.93 vs. the 52-week range of $1.58 to $18.60.

data.title: Ucloudlink expands global reach of PetPhone in partnership with Aham Al-Tafasil
data.description:
• Ucloudlink Group (NASDAQ:UCL) announced on Friday the procurement of a 30,000-unit order for PetPhone from Aham Al-Tafasil.
  • This partnership will accelerate PetPhone’s time-to-market, increase delivery speeds, enhance channel efficiency, and strengthen brand visibility throughout Saudi Arabia and the wider Gulf region, marking another significant step in PetPhone’s growing global presence.

data.title: Former McLaren chief eyed as potential Porsche CEO
data.description:
• Porsche’s (OTCPK:POAHY) executive committee has authorized the chairman of its supervisory board to enter talks with CEO Dr. Oliver Blume over a potential early, mutually agreed termination of his role on the executive board, the German sports car maker said.
  • Dr. Michael Leiters, former CEO of McLaren Automotive Limited, "is available as a potential successor" as chairman of the executive board. Leiters formerly worked at Porsche for 13 years, as well as at Italy's Ferrari.
  • Negotiations with Dr. Leiters will be initiated. Dr. Blume would continue to serve as the chairman of the executive board of Volkswagen AG (OTCPK:VWAGY), the company said.
  • The announcement follows Porsche’s (OTCPK:DRPRY) decision to slow its EV transition and delay the rollout of its all-electric SUV reverberated through its parent company, leading Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) (OTCPK:VWAPY) to lower its profit outlook for 2025.
  • Porsche AG (OTCPK:DRPRY) lowered its automotive EBITDA margin to 10.5% to 12.5% in September from an earlier forecast of 14.5% to 16.5%.

data.title: Grainger announces agreement to divest cormwell
data.description:
• Grainger (NYSE: NYSE:GWW) announced on Friday a definitive agreement to sell its U.K.-based Cromwell business to Aurelius, a global private equity investor.
  • The company also stated the proposed closure of its Zoro U.K. business, subject to the outcome of regulatory and employment consultation processes.
  • Following the transaction, the company would be expecting a one-time, non-cash after-tax loss of $190M-$205M, with the majority recorded in Q3 2025.

data.title: Gen Z protests: Peru set to declare state of emergency
data.description:
Gen Z protests against governments are spreading across countries, with the latest unrest calling for Peru's new president Jose Jeri to step down.

The Peruvian government is expected to declare a state of emergency soon, after overnight anti-government protests turned violent, with one person killed and hundreds injured.

Gen Z activists have led protests against corruption and rising crime in Peru over the past month. The protests led to the midnight ouster of former President Dina Boluarte last Thursday.

Jerí was sworn in as president soon after, but protesters have called for him to step down as well. Jerí has refused to resign.

Gen Z-led protests against corruption, economic failures, and poor employment and living conditions have broken out across the world this year.

These protests gained wider popularity following the unrest in Nepal last month, which forced its prime minister to resign. Its next leader was chosen through a poll on Discord - a messaging app popular with online gamers.

More recently, Madagascar's president was forced out of power this week. A military coup followed, with leader Michael Randrianirina being sworn in as president on Friday.

Gen Z, referring to people born roughly between 1996 and 2010, is the first generation to grow up entirely in the internet age.

"What connects these youth-led protests is a shared sense that traditional political systems aren't responsive to their generation's concerns, whether that's corruption, climate change, or economic inequality," said Sam Nadel, director of the nonprofit Social Change Lab.

-- The Associated Press contributed to this report.

data.title: Mawson Infrastructure Group reports prelim Q3 results
data.description:
• Mawson Infrastructure Group (NASDAQ:MIGI) expects preliminary revenues of approximately $11.2 million for the third quarter of 2025, a year-on-year decrease of 9%. Analysts project revenue of $5.3M for the quarter.
  • Preliminary net loss is expected to be around $1.5 million, compared with $12.2 million for the third quarter of 2024. Gross profit margin is expected to be 59%, compared with 35% last year.
  • MIGI --14.9% premarket to $1.46.
  • Source: Press Release
